note: Phase 3. Authored once, all five parts together, against the frozen 01_dossier.json. FROZEN after reject tests pass. Calibration bar = P3 published thesis (build plan §14).

theses.opener_register: reader-implicating epidemiology — opens on how common anomalous experience is, not on the diagnosis
theses.claim: Schizophrenia is not marked off from ordinary life by the presence of hallucinations or strange beliefs — those are common — but by their persistence, conviction, and cost; the diagnostic line is real, yet it is a threshold on a continuum, not a wall.
theses.why_this_claim: The general-population epidemiology (psychotic experiences at 5.8-7.2%, vastly above the ~0.7% morbid risk) forces a continuum reading. Naming that openly is more honest than the safe 'distinct brain disease unlike anything you experience', and it reframes every differential as a question of degree and duration, which is what the diagnostic-stability data actually show.

moves_omitted.reason: Part 2 carries the first-person account; repeating is the standalone tax paid twice.
Part 4 owns treatment; Part 1 must not drift toward what-to-do.

theses.the_risk: Stating the continuum openly — that the reader may already have had an experience on the same dimension — on the one condition where a reader most wants to self-diagnose, while rendering the criteria strictly as what clinicians weigh and refusing to hand over a checklist.

theses.opener_register: intimate first-person SCENE — a published verbatim account of psychosis from the inside
theses.claim: The symptoms that define schizophrenia in the public mind — voices and delusions — are the ones that matter least for how a life actually goes; the negative and cognitive symptoms nobody pictures are what predict whether someone works, studies, and connects.
theses.why_this_claim: Green 1996 found positive symptoms unassociated with functional outcome in every study reviewed; cognition sits ~1 SD below controls and negative symptoms out-predict all other domains for functioning (Rabinowitz/CATIE). The public picture and even the treatment conversation are aimed at the wrong symptoms.

moves_omitted.reason: Epidemiological weight is Part 1's; Part 2 is portrait, not counts.
Owned by Part 1; Part 2's two myths are auditory-only and insight-absent.

theses.the_risk: Asserting the asymmetry as fact and unhedged — that positive symptoms, which own the public image and most of the treatment conversation, are the weakest predictor of the outcomes people care about — where a cautious writer would soften it into 'all symptoms matter'.

theses.opener_register: interrogative / mechanistic — opens on a question (why late adolescence, almost never childhood?)
theses.claim: The 'split mind' folk-model is the exact inverse of the mechanism — this is a failure of integration, not a multiplication of selves.
theses.why_this_claim: This is the published calibration thesis (build plan §14). The convergent picture — protracted adolescent synaptic pruning, complement C4, 287 polygenic loci with no single gene, presynaptic dopamine that D2 drugs miss, NMDA hypofunction — is a story of a system failing to integrate, which is the precise opposite of the popular image of a mind split into personalities.

moves_omitted.reason: Part 2 carries it; omitting is deliberate (build plan).
No honest single chart exists (incommensurable effect sizes); reallocated to a second DiagramBlock at the data pass — logged.
The integration-vs-multiplication point IS this part's thesis, carried in prose/HighlightBox; the folk 'split personality = DID' correction is owned by Part 1.

theses.the_risk: Saying plainly that the dopamine hypothesis, taught as THE mechanism, is alone insufficient — D2 blockade barely touches negative and cognitive symptoms and glutamate/NMDA hypofunction is co-equal — and that there is no biomarker, no single gene, no one chain: a set of converging partial accounts, which is more interesting than a tidy hedge.

theses.opener_register: counter-expectation clinical finding — opens on a number that overturns what the reader expected (untreated time predicts outcome)
theses.claim: The most powerful things that help in schizophrenia are the least-discussed — coordinated care and family support rival medication for preventing relapse — and 'non-adherence' is usually a rational response to real side-effect burden, not a failure of will.
theses.why_this_claim: Antipsychotics genuinely more than halve one-year relapse (27% vs 64%), but family intervention (relapse RR 0.55) and coordinated specialty care (superior on all 13 outcomes) carry some of the largest, least-known effects, and non-adherence (44-56%) tracks a real, describable side-effect burden (TD ~25%, weight, EPS) — reframing it as rational, not moral.

moves_omitted.reason: Part 2/5 carry first-person; Part 4 is practice.
F-PRACTICE canonical order carries none; the ECT/institutionalisation correction is prose, not a myth block.

theses.the_risk: Taking two positions in the part with the most regulatory exposure: that under-discussed psychosocial care rivals medication for relapse, and that non-adherence is a rational response rather than a patient failing — both stated and cited — while never once naming a dose, a drug recommendation, or a second-person treatment instruction.

theses.opener_register: myth-correcting sociological — opens on a statistic that contradicts the reader's assumption about danger/outcome
theses.claim: Living well with schizophrenia is governed less by the illness itself than by things the word 'support' usually ignores — a body that dies about fifteen years early of preventable heart disease, a world that treats the victim as the threat, and a recovery rate real enough to matter and contested enough that no single number is honest.
theses.why_this_claim: The largest life-shortening force is preventable CVD (14.5-year gap; CVD the leading cause, accidents killing twice as many as suicide), the public fear is inverted (victimisation 20-34% vs perpetration 2-13%; ~5% population-attributable violence), and recovery is genuinely contested (13.5% strict/chronic vs 38% first-episode) — so 'support' must be redirected and the comforting number must be refused.
theses.the_risk: Stating the mortality gap and the violence-victimisation inversion plainly, and — hardest — refusing to resolve the recovery-rate contest by picking the comforting figure: giving the reader the real spread and the reason it is disputed, on the highest-stigma condition in the library.

SCENE.rule: First-person account lives in the two portrait/life parts; the other three omit to avoid the standalone tax.

split-personality-MythVsFact.rule: P1 owns the folk 'split personality = DID' correction; P3's integration-vs-multiplication is its thesis, in prose.

WEIGHT-epidemiology.rule: Incidence/prevalence/onset counts are P1's; P2 is portrait.

no_overlap_ledger.rule: Every statistic, study, MythVsFact, and image is claimed by exactly one part. Cross-part collisions in the raw research were resolved here.

resolved_collisions.note: P1 owns onset epidemiology (WEIGHT). P3's opener references the timing qualitatively and grounds WHY in Petanjek pruning + Weinberger, without re-citing Solmi's numbers.
P3 replaces its second MythVsFact with single-gene/purely-genetic (Trubetskoy + Caspi) to avoid duplicating P1.

P1: schizophrenia is not split/multiple personality (that is DID); Bleuler's schizo- = split among psychic functions
P2: hallucinations are not always auditory (Lim: 60-80% auditory, 53% multimodal)
insight is variable/fluctuating, not simply absent (Dickerson: ~half moderate, 25% severe)
P3: the schizophrenogenic mother (Hartwell: real theory, refuted)
there is no single 'schizophrenia gene' / it is not purely genetic (Trubetskoy 287 loci; Caspi G x E)
